records.ety: [L. architectura, fr. architectus: cf. F. architecture. See Architect.]

defs: The art or science of building; especially, the art of building houses, churches, bridges, and other structures, for the purposes of civil life; -- often called civil architecture.
Construction, in a more general sense; frame or structure; workmanship.
quotes: The architecture of grasses, plants, and trees.
The formation of the first earth being a piece of divine architecture.
